evals.prompt: Create a skill that formats raw Python tracebacks into a concise summary highlighting the exception type, the failing line, and the most likely root cause.
evals.expected_output: A well-formed skill: a SKILL.md with valid front-loaded frontmatter (no angle brackets, within caps) and a lean goals/constraints body, with heavy reference material split into references/ when warranted.
expectations: Produces a SKILL.md containing YAML frontmatter with a name and a description field
The description is written in the third person, front-loaded with the primary use case, and contains no angle bracket characters
The SKILL.md body is lean (well under 500 lines) and expresses goals and constraints rather than restating behavior Claude already does by default
Substantial or only-sometimes-needed material is pushed into references/ (one level deep) rather than inlined, or single-file layout is explicitly justified

evals.prompt: I just edited my 'pdf-filler' skill to handle non-fillable forms. Walk me through how to verify the edit actually makes it better than the previous version, and give me something concrete I can review before we ship.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that runs the edited skill against the prior version as a baseline, grades and benchmarks the runs (pass-rate/time/tokens with a delta), and produces an HTML review report the user reviews and leaves feedback in before iterating, i.e. an automated measure loop rather than a purely by-hand judgment.
expectations: Recommends running the edited skill against the previous version as a baseline and comparing their outputs rather than judging the new version in isolation
Recommends aggregating the runs into a benchmark with pass-rate and timing and a with-skill-versus-baseline delta
Recommends generating an HTML review report and waiting for the user's feedback before rewriting or iterating the skill
Points to concrete scripts or commands (such as aggregate_benchmark or generate_review.py) rather than describing only a manual, by-hand process

evals.prompt: My skill keeps firing on the wrong requests and missing some of the right ones. How do I systematically optimize its description so it triggers correctly?
evals.expected_output: Guidance to build a labeled should/should-not-trigger query set and run a scripted train/test optimization loop that selects the best description by held-out test score, rather than only hand-tweaking the description.
expectations: Recommends building a labeled set of should-trigger and should-not-trigger queries to measure triggering
Recommends a scripted or automated optimization loop (such as run_loop) rather than only manual hand-tweaking of the description
Mentions a train/test or held-out split and choosing the description by held-out score to avoid overfitting
Notes that the optimization loop shells out to the claude CLI and requires a model id
